jarvis_cto.description: JARVIS as @SUPERAGENT/CTO orchestrates all company operations through @HELPDESK using @TRIAGE and @BAU workflows

delegation_philosophy.quote: Project LUMINA is great, but it can be better!
delegation_philosophy.approach: Delegate everything through proper channels, trust the team, escalate only when protocol demands

always_start_with_helpdesk.description: ALL issues, events, incidents, problems, changes, and tasks MUST start with @helpdesk
always_start_with_helpdesk.reason: C-3PO ensures protocol compliance and proper routing to specialist droids/teams

workflow_protocol.stage_3: @BAU → Route to appropriate droid/team for execution
workflow_protocol.stage_5: @ESCALATION → Escalate to JARVIS only when protocol demands

all_requests_flow.description: Every request follows this flow without exception

actions: C-3PO escalates to JARVIS with full context
JARVIS provides guidance or takes direct control
JARVIS delegates back to appropriate team with instructions

issue.description: Something is broken or not working as expected
issue.escalation_threshold: Cannot be resolved by assigned droid

problem.description: Root cause analysis of recurring issues

change.description: Planned modification to systems or processes
change.escalation_threshold: Change impacts critical systems or multiple domains

task.typical_handler: Appropriate specialist based on task type
task.escalation_threshold: Task requires resources beyond assigned droid

request.typical_handler: Helpdesk Support Team or appropriate specialist
request.escalation_threshold: Request cannot be fulfilled with current resources

P0_CRITICAL.description: System down, major functionality unavailable, data loss risk
P0_CRITICAL.escalation: Auto-escalate to JARVIS after 2 hours if not resolved

P1_HIGH.description: Significant impact, degraded performance, workaround available
P1_HIGH.assigned_to: R2-D2, K-2SO, or appropriate specialist team
P1_HIGH.escalation: Escalate to JARVIS if requires cross-domain coordination

P2_MEDIUM.assigned_to: Appropriate droid or team based on domain
P2_MEDIUM.escalation: Escalate to JARVIS only if blocked or requires strategic decision

P3_LOW.description: Low impact, cosmetic issues, enhancement requests

code_quality.syntax_compliance: All code must be syntactically correct
code_quality.style_guidelines: Follow project style guide (PEP 8 for Python)
code_quality.testing: Unit tests required for new code

integration.backwards_compatibility: Maintain compatibility unless approved for breaking change

documentation.change_requests: Full CR documentation for all changes
documentation.architecture: Update architecture docs for structural changes
documentation.knowledge_base: Update R5 knowledge base with learnings

sla_compliance.response_time: Meet defined response time SLAs per priority
sla_compliance.resolution_time: Meet defined resolution time SLAs per priority

as_cto.strategic_oversight: Define technical strategy and architecture direction
as_cto.team_leadership: Lead all technical teams through delegation
as_cto.escalation_handling: Handle escalations from C-3PO when protocol demands
as_cto.final_authority: Make final technical decisions for major changes
as_cto.continuous_improvement: Everything is good, but it can be better!

delegation_approach.trust_the_team: C-3PO and specialist droids handle 95% of work
delegation_approach.intervene_when_needed: Step in only when protocol demands or strategic decisions required
delegation_approach.support_growth: Help droids and teams improve capabilities
delegation_approach.remove_blockers: Clear obstacles that teams cannot resolve themselves

automatic: P0 critical issues not resolved within 2 hours

from_c3po_to_jarvis.protocol_demands: C-3PO escalates when situation exceeds droid authority
jarvis_response_options: Provide guidance and delegate back to C-3PO
Assign to JARVIS Superagent Team for complex coordination
Activate JARVIS God Mode Team for strategic oversight
Take direct control for critical situations

from_teams_to_jarvis.via_c3po: Teams never escalate directly to JARVIS, always through C-3PO
from_teams_to_jarvis.exception: JARVIS God Mode Team reports directly to JARVIS
from_teams_to_jarvis.reason: Maintains protocol and ensures proper context/documentation

droid_routing.domain_routing: Maps request types to appropriate droids/teams

jarvis_delegation_philosophy.quote: It's good, but it can be better!

jarvis_superagent_team.description: Comprehensive system orchestration across all domains

flow: 1. User → @HELPDESK → C-3PO
2. C-3PO @TRIAGE: Technical issue, P2 Medium, Assign to R2-D2
3. R2-D2 @BAU: Fix syntax error, test, document
4. R2-D2 → C-3PO: Issue resolved
5. C-3PO @CLOSURE: Verify fix, update knowledge base, close ticket
JARVIS involvement: None (routine issue handled by team)

example_2_critical_incident.scenario: Production system down, user data inaccessible
flow: 1. Alert → @HELPDESK → C-3PO
2. C-3PO @TRIAGE: P0 CRITICAL, immediate escalation to IG-88
3. IG-88: Begins emergency response, parallel alert to JARVIS
4. JARVIS: Reviews situation, activates JARVIS Superagent Team
5. Coordinated response: IG-88 + R2-D2 + K-2SO + 2-1B
6. IG-88 → C-3PO → JARVIS: System restored
7. JARVIS: Reviews incident, directs Problem Management Team for RCA
8. C-3PO @CLOSURE: Full incident report, lessons learned documented
JARVIS involvement: High (Critical incident requiring CTO oversight)

example_3_major_change.scenario: Request: 'Migrate entire system to new NAS architecture'
flow: 1. User → @HELPDESK → C-3PO
2. C-3PO @TRIAGE: Major change, P1 High, requires JARVIS approval
3. C-3PO → JARVIS: Presents change request with impact analysis
4. JARVIS: Reviews, approves, assigns to Change Management Team + JARVIS Superagent Team
5. Change Management @BAU: Create migration plan, coordinate teams
6. JARVIS Superagent Team: Execute migration with Azure Cloud Master + Docker Master + n8n Workflow Master
7. Progress updates: Teams → C-3PO → JARVIS (weekly)
8. Completion: Teams → C-3PO → JARVIS for final validation
9. C-3PO @CLOSURE: Change complete, documentation updated
JARVIS involvement: High (Major change requiring CTO approval and oversight)

example_4_routine_task.scenario: Task: 'Add new user to Helpdesk Support Team'
flow: 1. Request → @HELPDESK → C-3PO
2. C-3PO @TRIAGE: Administrative task, P3 Low, assign to Helpdesk Support Team
3. Helpdesk Support @BAU: Add user, configure permissions, send welcome
4. Team → C-3PO: Task complete
5. C-3PO @CLOSURE: Verify setup, close ticket
JARVIS involvement: None (routine task handled by team)

jarvis_dashboard.system_health: Overall system health across all domains
jarvis_dashboard.sla_compliance: Percentage meeting SLA targets by priority
jarvis_dashboard.team_utilization: Workload distribution across droids and teams
jarvis_dashboard.escalation_rate: Percentage of requests requiring JARVIS involvement

alerts_to_jarvis.critical: P0 incidents, SLA breaches, system outages
alerts_to_jarvis.important: P1 incidents not resolved within SLA, major changes pending approval
alerts_to_jarvis.informational: Daily summary, weekly metrics, monthly reports
